item.title: Second alleged 'crew' charged over Bankstown woman Thi Kim Tran's kidnapping, murder
item.description: Further charges have been laid over the alleged kidnapping and murder of a western Sydney woman who police believe was targeted due to her husband's organised crime links.

item.title: PM says he's in contact with South-East Asian partners over fuel security — as it happened
item.description: The prime minister says he is in contact with South-East Asian partners over fuel security as the war in the Middle East continues.

item.title: Daly Cherry-Evans's return to Brookvale Oval a look into the past as Manly faces uncertain future
item.description: With Manly's season teetering on the edge, the Sea Eagles faithful welcomed former skipper Daly Cherry-Evans to Brookvale Oval in enemy colours for the first time. But it was the prodigal son who won the day.

item.title: Almost one in three eligible voters in Coober Pedy were unable to cast a ballot
item.description: Nearly one in three Coober Pedy voters, or more than 250 people, missed their chance to vote in the state election last week after the town was left without a polling booth on election day.

item.title: ASX shares in lacklustre territory as markets focus on Middle East war duration — as it happened
item.description: Australian shares finished broadly flat today. The Australian dollar managed to breach 69 US cents, but trading was lacklustre. For more on all of the ramifications, see how the day unfolded on our business blog.

item.title: Mudgee Medical Centre secures six doctors to reopen books after 18 months
item.description: A medical centre in Central West NSW is accepting new patients for the first time in 18 months, after community support helped attract six new doctors.

item.title: Petition calls for release of police video of Indigenous man's death in custody
item.description: Raylene Nixon says the public should be able to make their own minds up about what happened to her son in his final moments.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle smashes west Pilbara coast, tearing off roofs in Exmouth
item.description: Residents on WA's north-west coast are feeling the full brunt of severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it barrels across the region, tearing off roofs and flooding streets, with the system now downgraded to a category three.

item.title: Wind power off Victoria's coast could reduce need for new transmission lines, report finds
item.description: Developing Victoria's wind power sector could save the state money and powerline infrastructure, while providing stability for Eastern Australia's power grid, a new report shows.

item.title: Boat master jailed over illegally caught sea cucumbers in Far North Queensland
item.description: Authorities found almost two tonnes of the lucrative sea cucumbers on board a foreign fishing boat that was hidden in mangroves near a remote river in the country's far north earlier this month.

item.title: Goulburn community solar farm plugs in after 12-year journey
item.description: Hundreds of residents of a rural NSW town combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

item.title: Cape York stations hit by Cyclone Narelle call for army's help to clean up
item.description: Floodwaters may have receded around the Archer River but the clean-up is just beginning after Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle tore through last week.

item.title: Big Red Bash cancelled for 2026, outback tourism facing uncertain season as fuel prices bite
item.description: This festival usually sees people go all out, spending thousands of dollars and travelling thousands of kilometres, but a potential change in location and fuel concerns have deterred ticket holders.

item.title: How Australians interact with birds and wildlife around their homes
item.description: Our recent stories about attracting native fauna to gardens and feeding wild birds resonated strongly. Here is how some Australians interact with wildlife in their local areas.

item.title: 'QuitGPT' boycott could spark AI flashpoint as ethical use debate rages
item.description: AI companies are stuck in a "perfect storm" of trying to control the use and output of their platforms while maintaining competitive profits, as millions of people boycott the technology, experts say.

item.title: 'Surreal' feeling as snow falls in March across parts of New South Wales
item.description: It is still only March, but snow is falling in parts of NSW and the Bureau of Meteorology is checking its record books.

item.title: Enriched uranium hidden deep in Iranian mountains could be key to ending war
item.description: A move from Israel or the United States to find and secure Iran's enriched uranium stockpile would be dangerous, but experts say it is one of the only ways to end the war and prevent it from returning.

item.title: Agencies warn elderly could suffer as fuel price rises hit aged care services
item.description: The national peak body for aged care providers is calling on the federal government to provide urgent support to keep services running, as the Middle East war sends fuel prices skyrocketing.

item.title: More than 1,000 Australians physically assaulted every day, new ABS crime data shows
item.description: Almost 400,000 Australians experienced physical assault in 2024-2025, according to new victimisation data released by the Australian Bureau of Statistics.

item.title: Gladstone's Jake Lyle to star in Phantom of the Opera on Sydney Harbour
item.description: Off sick from school, it was "love at first sight" for Jake Lyle the first time he saw Phantom of the Opera. Now the central Queensland talent is playing his dream role as Phantom on Sydney Harbour.

item.title: Mould infection cluster report identifies RPA balcony as likely exposure site, one patient remains in ICU
item.description: A balcony at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital has been identified as the infection site of a mould cluster linked to two patient deaths late last year.

item.title: PM leaves door open to halving fuel excise temporarily
item.description: The prime minister leaves the door open to halving taxes on petrol and diesel, after the Coalition demands temporary relief from sky-high fuel prices.

item.title: Conflict reshapes Persian New Year for Iranian Australians
item.description: As Iranian Australians try to celebrate Persian new year, news of the conflict in the Middle East is always front of mind.

item.title: Calls for government support after severe weather floods Kakadu outstations
item.description: Kakadu residents whose homes have been submerged by roof-high floodwater said they feel like they've been forgotten, with evacuation centres and relief payments not made available to them.

item.title: Sheffield Shield final: Victoria's top order wobbles as South Australia misses bonus point chance
item.description: Victoria and South Australia meet in the final of the Sheffield Shield at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

item.title: Fuel surcharges across a broad range of industries are adding to consumers' financial stress
item.description: Fuel surcharges are being applied across a range of industries, from the delivery of bricks to building sites to Australia's biggest rideshare companies, meaning consumer prices are rising.

item.title: Former ALP politician Milton Orkopoulos plans appeal against sex abuse convictions
item.description: Paedophile and former NSW Labor politician Milton Orkopoulos says he has Legal Aid funding to explore an appeal against his convictions for abusing seven boys.

item.title: Can states cap fuel prices? Tasmanian emergency fuel laws explained
item.description: The Tasmanian government is seeking to have the power to cap petrol prices. But how will it work? And if it does, can other states do the same?

item.title: Anti-logging protester Ruth Langford/Tipruthanna requests court hearing on Tasmanian Aboriginal land
item.description: A member of the Tasmanian Aboriginal community charged with trespass has applied to the Hobart Magistrates Court to have her case heard on Aboriginal-owned land.

item.title: Vision of heavy winds in Onslow
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects
item.description: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

item.title: AFL admits Adelaide wronged by crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Geelong
item.description: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

item.title: New Spirit of Tasmania ferry to be Dark Mofo 2026 floating art gallery
item.description: The new spirit of Tasmania ferries might not be available for journeys across the Bass Strait yet — but this winter, one of them will take on a new role as an art gallery, during Hobart's Dark Mofo festival.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle heads inland amid reports of widespread damage in Exmouth
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle continues to cut a destructive swathe through Western Australia's Gascoyne even as it weakens, with authorities warning the extreme weather is not over yet.

item.title: Agnes Water man charged with 3D printing firearm parts following police investigation
item.description: Police say the charges come amid a concerning rise in the use of 3D printing technology to manufacture weapons.

item.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries
item.description: More than 500 AFL players will soon no longer be covered by insurance for brain injuries in their superannuation after Zurich Insurance moved to cut their ability to claim for concussion and head trauma.

item.title: Authorities urge NSW beachgoers to cancel plans amid hazardous weather, surf conditions
item.description: New South Wales beaches will be too dangerous to visit this weekend, with authorities issuing a warning to rock fishers, swimmers and boaters to stay away from the coastline.

item.title: Diesel fuel stolen after thieves break into rural property in central Tasmania
item.description: Several hundred litres of diesel fuel have been stolen during an overnight break-in on a rural property in central Tasmania.

item.title: Queensland opposition says government broke promise on adult crime adult time advice
item.description: A report from the expert legal panel advising the state government's adult crime, adult time policy has been released, explaining the framework for the advice, rather than everything provided to the government.

item.title: Kelpie with head wounds found tied to tree at Adelaide Hills golf course
item.description: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information. Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

item.title: Broncos claim pressure-relieving victory over Dolphins, ending tough week on a high
item.description: The Broncos overpowered the Dolphins 26-22 in the Battle of Brisbane, after Wests Tigers stunned ladder leaders New Zealand Warriors in Auckland. Follow live.

item.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues
item.description: The SA government and AFL are in discussions to try to keep fans flocking to Adelaide for next month's AFL Gather Round, as the global fuel crisis threatens to disrupt travel plans.

item.title: Magpies confirm 'hamstring awareness' for Moore after win over Giants
item.description: Collingwood is expecting to be without its skipper Darcy Moore and his predecessor Scott Pendlebury next week against Brisbane after the pair were hobbled during the win over GWS.

item.title: Proposed new laws allow police to move people on for 'causing anxiety' without committing a crime
item.description: Young people could be moved on by police officers for "causing anxiety" without ever committing a crime under proposed Queensland laws.

item.title: Friday Finance with Dan Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Six-day Telstra outage in remote NT community of Kintore impacting Triple Zero calls
item.description: Residents in the remote NT community of Kintore have had difficulty making Triple Zero calls during a six-day Telstra outage, which has also caused issues buying fuel.

item.title: Manly sacks coach Anthony Seibold after three games of 2026 season
item.description: Manly has sacked head coach Anthony Seibold after a dismal start to his fourth NRL season at the helm.

item.title: Socceroos snatch late win against Cameroon as Jordy Bos delivers and teenager Lucas Herrington impresses
item.description: Jordy Bos hits the back of the net as the Socceroos leave it late against Cameroon in Sydney with 18-year-old Lucas Herrington impressing on debut.

item.title: Ukraine and Saudi Arabia sign 'mutually beneficial' air defence deal
item.description: The deal comes at a crucial time for Kyiv, as US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has said weapons for Ukraine could be diverted to the Iran war

item.title: Trump struggles to find an off-ramp as an oil crisis meets a munitions crisis
item.description: While markets struggle to price a sudden collapse in oil supply, military analysts are sounding the alarm on the rate countries are burning through weapons stockpiles.

item.title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle still packing strong winds as system moves inland
item.description: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakens as it travels south through WA's Wheatbelt, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.

item.title: Best books out in March, from Francis Spufford, Asako Yuzuki, Laura McCluskey and more
item.description: Wondering what to read next? Gothic crime fiction, a cult Japanese bestseller and a fantastical twist on the Blitz are among our critics' latest recommendations.

item.title: From Robyn to Courtney Barnett — these are the best albums of March
item.description: The year might've kicked off with a flood of great records, but March is when things really snap into gear.

item.title: Why the end of the Iran war could be just the start of Australia's involvement
item.description: The federal government is turning its mind to what Australian support in the Middle East might look like after a ceasefire.

item.title: Claims gas exporters on 'best behaviour' as government intervention looms
item.description: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

item.title: TGA head defends handling of Australia's sunscreen scandal over SPF test results
item.description: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

item.title: Online travel site Guest Reservations accused of fleecing customers with hidden fees and charges
item.description: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

item.title: The community behind Geelong music festival Jerkfest's unlikely success
item.description: As music festivals collapse, one Geelong kid's hobby has turned into a big day out.

item.title: Months out from Thriving Kids, workforce questions remain unanswered
item.description: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

item.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer — yet most still miss the early signs
item.description: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

item.title: Pakistan's conflict with Afghan Taliban intensifies in shadow of Middle East war
item.description: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

item.title: Growing pains as blueberry industry expands rapidly and appetites increase
item.description: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.

item.title: Yemen's Houthis launch first missiles on Israel as Middle East war intensifies
item.description: The Iranian-backed militant group took to X to make the announcement, saying it launched a salvo of ballistic rockets at military targets in southern Israel.

item.title: NSW Waratahs snap losing streak with Super Rugby win over ACT Brumbies in Canberra
item.description: NSW Waratahs coach Dan McKellar heralds his side's much-needed 30-28 victory against bitter rivals the ACT Brumbies as a special memory for the club.

item.title: Katherine residents call for hospital relocation after flood evacuations
item.description: After Katherine's worst flood in 28 years, residents are calling for governments to act on a decades-old idea to relocate the NT town's hospital away from the flood-prone river.

item.title: Treaty elections underway in Victoria as changes flow from nation-leading agreement
item.description: Victoria's treaty elections are underway, with successful candidates taking on new responsibilities. But uncertainty remains, with the opposition sticking to its pledge to scrap the agreement if elected in November.

item.title: Australian Iranians hit back over 'racist' decision to block 7,000 tourist visa holders
item.description: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

item.title: Iran's soccer team honours victims of deadly airstrike on primary school
item.description: Iran soccer players hold small backpacks to honour the victims of a deadly missile strike on a girls' primary school, before a friendly against Nigeria.

item.title: Hawksbill sea turtle returns to ocean after months-long recovery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Critically endangered hawksbill sea turtle returns to wild months after beach rescue
item.description: What passers-by thought was a "sandy rock" was in fact a critically endangered turtle far from home.

item.title: Measles on the rise across NSW, concern community infections spreading undetected
item.description: An outbreak of measles in south-east Asia has fuelled a surge in NSW cases — but the state's biggest cause for concern is five infections believed to have been spread undetected in the community.

item.title: The strange death of Liberal England and the lessons for Liberal Adelaide
item.description: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

item.title: Running influencers are making big money and inspiring many to get active — but there are risks
item.description: Recently, online discussion has turned to what some perceive to be a spate of influencers getting injured.

item.title: Military action lights up skies in Jerusalem, Israel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: End of an era for Tharwa General Store after a century being owned by one family
item.description: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

item.title: Critical bridges across Lebanon's Litani River targeted in calculated invasion plan
item.description: Israel's expanded invasion of southern Lebanon was at least 12 days in the making, with bridges targeted to effectively cut off the south from the country's centre.

item.title: Roger Tuivasa-Sheck signs for English club Wakefield Trinity, to leave Warriors at end of NRL season
item.description: Just hours after the Warriors were beaten for the first time this NRL season, the Kiwi club have suffered another surprise setback.

item.title: Toowoomba Royal Show in full swing despite soaring fuel costs
item.description: Showmen are determined to keep putting on agricultural shows for regional Australia despite the "frightening" cost of fuel.

item.title: Isolated school students face difficult decisions amid surging fuel prices
item.description: Ruben Driver has a round trip of 4,000 kilometres to reach his boarding school. The cost of fuel is placing his family under immense pressure to get him to class.

item.title: Victoria in the box seat against South Australia in Sheffield Shied final with first-innings points
item.description: Victoria is closing on the Sheffield Shield title after a dramatic day's play against South Australia, punctuated by confusion around an injury substitution.

item.title: EPA, developer defend approach amid mounting criticism over Smiths Beach green light
item.description: The City of Busselton says the watchdog has ignored its own rules after giving the Smiths Beach development the green light, but Fini Group says its work was "rigorous".

item.title: WA Minister Hannah Beazley flags more reforms for local council laws ahead of yet another election
item.description: Local Government Minister Hannah Beazley says reforms are needed to prevent local councils from quitting just a few months into the job.

item.title: Manly Sea Eagles premiership winner Kieran Foran named interim coach
item.description: Club legend Kieran Foran is appointed Manly's interim coach, after the Sea Eagles sacked Anthony Seibold just three games into the 2026 NRL season.

item.title: Timelapse of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's path
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wall Street drops again for a fifth straight week as Iran war uncertainty lingers
item.description: US stocks have deepened their drops as Wall Street finished off a fifth straight week, its longest such streak in nearly four years.

item.title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle disrupts WA's domestic gas supply, port operations
item.description: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

item.title: Government to underwrite fuel, fertiliser shipments
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How an Irukandji jellyfish the size of a fingernail causes 'unimaginable pain'
item.description: A tiny jellyfish found in Queensland has venom so powerful it causes feelings of "impending doom".

item.title: 'Not acceptable': Xerri facing ban after rubbing blood on Dons skipper's face
item.description: North Melbourne survives a nervy finish to break a decade-long drought against Essendon as star ruckman Tristan Xerri rubs blood on Andrew McGrath's face in a heated exchange.

item.title: Albanese’s new fuel powers ‘sensible thing to do’: Tony Wood
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Trump's new tourism envoy once proposed banishing pigeons from Sydney streets
item.description: US President Donald Trump has named Nick Adams as his new special presidential envoy for tourism. It isn't the Australian's first time in government.

item.title: Huge swells, hazardous surf batter NSW south coast beaches causing damage and erosion
item.description: Huge swells have inundated NSW beaches as most of the coastline remains closed to the public due to hazardous surf conditions.

item.title: Rylan Wooster's record-breaking run.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Not-for-profit steps in to bridge digital divide in remote Ngalingkadji Indigenous community
item.description: Residents from remote Ngalingkadji in Western Australia's north are being connected to the internet at home for the first time, bringing banking services, telehealth and emergency services.

item.title: Brown butter, oat and chocolate biscuits that are ready in no time - ABC News
item.description: Salty, sweet, chocolatey and buttery, these cookies are a Frankenstein melding of the best kind.

item.title: Two teenagers in custody, two on the run and paramedic injured after Melbourne crash
item.description: Police say four teenagers in a stolen car collided with an older couple in Carlton this morning, with two fleeing the scene immediately.

item.title: PM announces new powers to boost fuel supply amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The Albanese government will use public money to underwrite the delivery of extra cargoes of fuel in a bid to shore up supply during the Middle East conflict.

item.title: In one of the world's most visited countries, the local population is vanishing
item.description: Greece's population could drop by as much as 20 per cent by 2050, with the government declaring the situation a "national threat".

item.title: Australia women defeat West Indies by 103 runs in first ODI
item.description: Australia power to a 103-run win over the West Indies, despite Stafanie Taylor's breakthrough one-day century.

item.title: Cowboys score three tries in eight minutes to complete comeback against Storm
item.description: The Cowboys completed a second half comeback, scoring three tries in eight minutes, to defeat the Storm 28-24.

item.title: Services Australia urges patients to check if they are owed some of $272 million from Medicare
item.description: Services Australia is urging people to check if they're owed money from a $272 million pool of unclaimed money.

item.title: Bank of America agrees to pay $105 million to settle Epstein-related lawsuit
item.description: The Bank of America has agreed to pay $US72.5 million ($105.3 million) to settle a civil lawsuit brought by women who accused the bank of facilitating their sexual abuse by Jeffrey Epstein, court records show.

item.title: Lambs sold for $500 each as national flock dwindles towards record low
item.description: Australian sheep and lamb prices keep rising despite global uncertainty. But how long will consumers be willing to pay?

item.title: Cyclone Narelle isolates Exmouth, destroys airport and cuts essential services
item.description: The northern WA tourist town of Exmouth has been left isolated and heavily damaged after Cyclone Narelle tore through the town.

item.title: Man charged after baby dies following rideshare crash in Lynwood, two others seriously injured
item.description: A seven-month-old child has died after a rideshare vehicle crashed into a tree in Perth's south-east, leaving two other passengers seriously injured.

item.title: St John Ambulance NT reaches 'critical failure point' during 'extraordinary event' in Darwin
item.description: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

item.title: Oscar Piastri qualifies third at Japanese GP for McLaren as Kimi Antonelli claims pole
item.description: Australia's Oscar Piastri of McLaren qualifies third fastest for the Japanese Grand Prix, behind the Mercedes duo of Kimi Antonelli and George Russell.

item.title: Albanese government walks AI tightrope between risk and opportunity
item.description: As an artificial intelligence revolution approaches, the government is responding to a huge opportunity in data-centre development while balancing copyright concerns and workforce anxiety.

item.title: In a quest for an affordable mortgage my family tried apartment living
item.description: In a quest for an affordable mortgage in our location of choice, my family decide to rent an apartment as a trial. Will high-rise living be a realistic alternative to a house as a place to bring up our child?

item.title: The Offbeat Sari challenges cliches made about the 'traditional' and 'uncomfortable' sari
item.description: For many South Asians, museums in the West have historically been places where their cultures were displayed as static artefacts. An exhibition like The Offbeat Sari has flipped that script, presenting South Asian creativity as innovative and globally influential.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Looking for trivia glory? You've come to the wrong place
item.description: We know you've come here looking for trivia glory but you're unlikely to find any today.

item.title: Obongjayar on defying genre stereotypes, touring Australia and Little Simz
item.description: The Nigerian-British musician brightens up dark times, whether it's his adventurous solo music or scene-stealing collabs with Fred again.. and Little Simz.

item.title: In the Strait of Hormuz, ships make a bargain to negotiate their safe passage
item.description: Iran is running one of the world's most critical shipping lanes as a "toll booth" and has likely made hundreds of millions of dollars of extra income from selling its own oil since the US and Israel waged war against the regime.

item.title: Australia has to fight back against misinformation about climate change, Senate report says
item.description: Australia needs to increase its efforts to fight back against misinformation about climate change, a Senate report says.

item.title: Booking.com under fire as hundreds of complaints lodged with Fair Trading
item.description: Hundreds of complaints against Booking.com are piling up as customers and hosts allege they have lost money, their property has been trashed and no-one is taking responsibility.

item.title: Hassan's family is in hospital after an Israeli airstrike, but one child will not return home
item.description: Hassan Ali Al Jabal's family were all injured in an Israeli airstrike on their home in Lebanon. He does not know how to tell them one of his daughters is dead.

item.title: Here's what a month of fuel hikes looks like across Australia
item.description: From crop farmers to wildlife rescuers, here are some of the stories ABC readers told us about how the fuel crisis is impacting them.

item.title: Ask the Leyland Brothers' Mal Leyland planning 'one last trip' as he reveals dementia diagnosis
item.description: Adventurer and filmmaker Mal Leyland, of Ask the Leyland Brothers fame, shares his private health battle. But the TV legend is determined to retrace the cross-country trip that made him famous 60 years ago.

item.title: Iran warns US against ground invasion as key diplomats meet in Pakistan for talks
item.description: Iran's parliament speaker says Iran is prepared to confront any American forces on its soil and would set troops "on fire" in its warning against a US ground invasion.

item.title: How men ‘marrying up’ became the norm
item.description: For the first time, more men than women are "marrying up" the education ladder, toppling a millennia-old tradition, an exclusive ABC analysis has revealed.

item.title: Why Chinese student numbers are expected to take 'a sharp turn for the worse' in Australia
item.description: Chinese students continue to provide the lion's share of revenue for Australian universities. But experts warn that could soon change.

item.title: This 'one stop shop' for families says its model could be scaled up for Thriving Kids
item.description: While serious workforce questions surround the new Thriving Kids scheme, the organisers of an existing program in Victoria believe its model could be scaled up.

item.title: Gout Gout beaten by Lachlan Kennedy in 200 metres at Maurie Plant Meet in Melbourne
item.description: In an identical performance to their titanic battle 12 months ago, Lachlan Kennedy edges out Gout Gout to win the men's 200 metres at the Maurie Plant Meet in Melbourne.

item.title: AFL Victoria lawyers ask former footballer Daniel Hayes how much he drank before he was allegedly raped
item.description: Daniel Hayes was 17 years old when he was allegedly raped by his coach while playing in an Australian Rules football talent league in Victoria.

item.title: Senegal defies Confederation of African Football, parading disputed Africa Cup of Nations trophy in friendly victory in Paris
item.description: Senegal defies the decision to strip it of the Africa Cup of Nations trophy by parading it around the pitch before a 2-0 victory over Peru at the Stade de France in Paris.

item.title: Primary producers sell assets, consider lay-offs as fuel costs hit hard
item.description: The sudden rise in fuel costs is forcing farmers to make difficult decisions, such as laying off workers and selling equipment to keep their businesses running.

item.title: Free public transport in Victoria through April as fuel prices continue to climb
item.description: The Victorian government says public transport will be free across the state in April in a bid to relieve cost-of-living pressure as fuel prices continue to climb.

item.title: Coalition, One Nation idea would be 'absolute disaster' for Victoria
item.description: Former premier Steve Bracks says One Nation teaming up with the Coalition would be a disaster for Victoria, and has hit out at Jeff Kennett for even suggesting it.

item.title: Locations of two new shark listening stations for Sydney Harbour revealed
item.description: Sydney's lower north shore and eastern suburbs are likely locations for two new shark listening devices following a spate of attacks earlier this year.

item.title: Regional WA businesses say it's a matter of weeks before fuel prices start hitting consumers
item.description: Country business owners say they have worked to absorb price increases, but will be forced to pass on costs to consumers if the supply of fuel to regional WA isn't stabilised.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle destroys WA banana crops, Carnarvon growers hit hard
item.description: Fruit and vegetable producers in one of Western Australia's key food growing regions are counting the cost of Cyclone Narelle, with one banana grower reporting more than 80 per cent of his crop has been destroyed.

item.title: Indigenous families in NSW to receive more support to prevent child removals in $350m funding boost
item.description: The team of case workers at Tharawal Aboriginal Corporation in south-west Sydney helping to prevent children from going into foster care is about to double in size.

item.title: Showbags a highlight of sideshow alley for generations of Australians
item.description: The "uniquely Australian" tradition of the showbag dates back more than a century, and shows no sign of dying out.

item.title: State library showcases photo archive of trailblazing Indigenous journalist Wayne Coolwell
item.description: Journalist and broadcaster Wayne Coolwell documented years of Indigenous culture with his 35mm camera. The photographs are going on showcase at the State Library.

item.title: Brisbane couple give up city life, careers, and move to outback to run Silverton Hotel
item.description: Burnt out from city life and careers in Brisbane, Liz Murphy and Norm Matau knew they couldn't keep going. So they left it all behind and started a new life as outback publicans.

item.title: Magistrate accuses ACT police and prosecutors of 'egregious conduct' over botched prosecution
item.description: Lawyers for a Canberra man cleared of 14 family violence charges are calling for an apology and compensation, after police found evidence the complainant fabricated some claims.

item.title: 15-year-old Tasmanian cricketer Mia Barwick set to ride wave of women's sporting boom
item.description: Mia Barwick is just 15 and a multi-sport prodigy, bound for cricketing glory. She's part of the next generation of female sporting stars — but with that comes pressure of the spotlight.

item.title: Houthis open up new front in Middle East war, making things much more complex for Trump
item.description: One month into the Middle East conflict, the fighting has expanded again with another new front opening up as the Houthis launched their first attack. It creates further complexity for Donald Trump who now has to contain a widening conflict, writes Allyson Horn.

item.title: Kyra Cooney-Cross to miss rest of season, Steph Catley and Caitlin Foord fire for Arsenal in WSL derby thrashing
item.description: Kyra Cooney-Cross won't play for Arsenal again this season as she stays in Australia to be with her sick mum, but Caitlin Foord is back firing for the Gunners.

item.title: Scientists 'crack the code' as endangered palm cockatoo chick hatches in artificial nest
item.description: Scientists rejoice as adult birds enter an artificial hollow to feed and incubate the egg, before hatching and eventually flying away.

item.title: Southern greater gliders emerging from artificial hollow
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Leigh Hoffman and Alessia McCaig complete track nationals sprint doubles in keirin
item.description: Leigh Hoffman and Alessia McCaig complete sprint doubles with victory in the keirin at the national track championships, with Hoffman eyeing a chance to one day compete in the lucrative Japanese keirin racing.

item.title: Why the 'perfect pair' of jeans remains elusive
item.description: The enduring challenge of finding the "perfect pair" of jeans highlights the garment's cultural significance, and the opportunity to move toward more sustainable and more thoughtfully designed denim.

item.title: South Australia claims late wickets against Victoria on day four of Sheffield Shield final
item.description: South Australia strikes twice late on day four to snatch the momentum from Victoria in the Sheffield Shield final at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

item.title: Andrew Hastie says Iran war a 'huge miscalculation' by Donald Trump
item.description: Coalition frontbencher Andrew Hastie has warned the credibility of the United States is being damaged and says Australians may be losing faith in the alliance.

item.title: Israeli military releases vision of strike on Lebanese journalists
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'No Kings' protests against Donald Trump draw crowds across US and Europe
item.description: Huge crowds rally against US President Donald Trump from coast to coast in what organisers hope will be the largest single-day protest in US history.

item.title: Tasmanian government makes public transport free until July as fuel prices rise
item.description: The Tasmanian government says public transport will be free statewide for three months, starting on Monday, in response to surging fuel prices.

item.title: Aftermath of Israeli strike on media vehicle in southern Lebanon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: A secret stash and history mystery solved — why Birrundudu drawings are rewriting timeline of Aboriginal art
item.description: A stash of more than 800 mysterious drawings has been revealed for the first time, rewriting the timeline of Aboriginal art and reconnecting families with long-lost relatives.

item.title: Demons overwhelm Blues in late MCG surge
item.description: The Demons complete an amazing comeback and pile pressure on Carlton coach Michael Voss, while West Coast celebrates an inspirational victory on the road against Port Adelaide.

item.title: Bullara Station feels 'intense' full force of category four Tropical Cyclone Narelle near Exmouth
item.description: An outback cattle station and tourist ranch in WA's north has felt the full force of Cyclone Narelle, with vision emerging of the trail of destruction left by the powerful wind and heavy rain.

item.title: When a connection feels like home
item.description: After cancelling a full Sri Lankan wedding and a string of "disastrous" relationships, Sashi Perera found herself opposite a man who made believing in him simple.

item.title: Annaliese Holland will die within months. Here is what she wants you to know
item.description: Before ending her life through voluntary assisted dying, Annaliese Holland shares her final wishes and advice on living a full life.

item.title: Donald Trump miscalculated the risks of war with Iran, Andrew Hastie says.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Underwater tour of shark listening station
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How AI is changing the price of white-collar jobs
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Piastri second as ill-timed safety car hands Mercedes teen another win
item.description: An ill-timed safety car has denied Oscar Piastri a stunning Japanese Grand Prix victory, with Italian Kimi Antonelli benefiting to secure successive wins.

item.title: Australia's oldest living Olympian Gordon Ingate celebrates 100th birthday
item.description: Gordon "Wingnut" Ingate was twice denied time off work to compete in the Olympic Games in his 20s, but continued to excel as a sailor, eventually getting the chance to compete in his 40s at Munich 1972.

item.title: Labor's Toby Priest defeats Liberals' Stephen Patterson to win Morphett in SA election
item.description: Liberal frontbencher Stephen Patterson loses his bid to be re-elected in the beachside seat of Morphett in the South Australian state election.

item.title: Free public transport ruled out for NSW commuters as Liberals, union renew calls for cost-of-living relief
item.description: The NSW Liberals and the Rail, Tram and Bus Union (RTBU) have renewed calls for free public transport in response to surging fuel prices.

item.title: Shane Flanagan on the challenge of coaching on after son's scary neck injury
item.description: St George Illawarra five-eighth Kyle Flanagan was transported to hospital after a head knock in the Dragons' 22-14 loss to the Gold Coast Titans.

item.title: 'Gate of Tears' at risk of becoming second major oil bottleneck
item.description: The opening of a new front in the Middle East war has put another vital shipping route on edge. Known in Arabic as the "Gate of Tears", analysts warn this narrow waterway could soon live up to its name.

item.title: Victorian Liberals dump Moira Deeming in upper house preselection contest
item.description: Controversial Victorian MP Moira Deeming has lost her preselection battle for the state election, with Liberal Party members replacing her with business leader Dinesh Gourisetty.

item.title: Kangaroos' Tristan Xerri sent to AFL tribunal after alleged blood-wiping incident
item.description: North Melbourne ruckman Tristan Xerri is sent directly to the AFL tribunal on a charge of serious misconduct after he allegedly wiped blood on Essendon captain Andy McGrath's face.

item.title: Australia to support NASA's Artemis II as first crewed Moon mission in 50 years launches
item.description: Canberra-based communications systems will support NASA's first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century, helping astronauts orbit the Moon and return data.

item.title: WA Premier announces emergency relief as ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle cleanup begins
item.description: Structures have been destroyed and more than 1000 people remain without power as the cleanup begins in Western Australia following ex-tropical cyclone Narelle.

item.title: Adelaide 'deployment exercise' tests emergency services with details drawn from recent events
item.description: South Australian emergency services conduct their largest mass casuality exercise in history in a bid to test coordination between agencies.

item.title: Sydney Kings defeat Adelaide 36ers 106-93 in NBL championship game three
item.description: The Sydney Kings edge a step closer to winning a sixth NBL championship following a 106-93 defeat of the Adelaide 36ers in front of a record crowd.

item.title: F1 Japanese Grand Prix quick hits: Oliver Bearman crash, Kimi Antonelli sets more records
item.description: Oliver Bearman limped away from a big crash after he needed to take evasive action. Here are the quick hits from the F1 Japanese Grand Prix.

item.title: From Australian Idol to The Piano, Guy Sebastian has tried most types of reality TV
item.description: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

item.title: You, Me & Tuscany starring Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page marks return of the romcom
item.description: You, Me & Tuscany is highly anticipated by fans of Halle Bailey and Regé-Jean Page, who can't wait for a rare romantic comedy to hit cinemas.

item.title: This week in history quiz: Are you ready to go back in time?
item.description: Cast your mind back and see if you can conquer this week's round of trivia.

item.title: How Australia became hostage to fuel imports
item.description: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

item.title: How housing costs are creating an 'almost hidden crisis' for single parents
item.description: Hunter was plunged into homelessness when his family lost their rental. It is an increasingly similar story to other single parents finding themselves "lifelong renters".

item.title: Easter trip plans cancelled over petrol price hikes driven by Middle East war
item.description: Rising fuel prices driven by the Middle East conflict are hitting the domestic tourism sector, with cancellations surging ahead of the Easter long weekend.

item.title: Australian fears about Iran war, fuel shortages run deeper than an anti-Trump attitude
item.description: The pandemic demonstrated that when global events become local, Australians are quick to turn on even a mighty trading partner. There is a real capacity here for the war to sour our attitude to the US alliance.

item.title: Health insurers are expanding into GP services. Here's why it could be a threat to Medicare
item.description: Major insurers like Medibank Private and Bupa are buying up GP clinics in a move that could lead to US-style managed care.

item.title: Universities' $1.8b spend on consultants and contractors shocks experts and politicians
item.description: Consultancies have been accused of infiltrating universities and wasting scarce public funds on questionable advice about cutting courses and jobs.

item.title: Adriana Rivas, the Bondi nanny accused of torture for Pinochet, makes last stand to evade extradition to Chile
item.description: Adriana Rivas has been a fugitive in Australia since fleeing Chile while on bail 20 years ago. A federal court hearing will determine whether she is removed to face trial for the alleged crimes of torture and kidnapping, committed on behalf of the Pinochet regime.

item.title: How to approach your employer about working from home and flexible work arrangements
item.description: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

item.title: Randwick City Council calls for ban on commercial student accommodation
item.description: For every 10 student rooms being built, there is only one residential property in an area of Sydney's east. The mayor says the housing market has been "monopolised".

item.title: Brisbane councillors blurred during meeting livestream
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Local government politicians blurred during Brisbane City Council meeting livestream
item.description: The incident sparked a debate over conspiracies, technology, TikTok, and clout-chasing in Australia's largest local council.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces has been forced to admit it edited a photo of Ali Shoeib to show him in a military uniform.

item.title: Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub closing due to lack of funding, leaving First Nations patients in Canberra in the lurch
item.description: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

item.title: Kylie Minogue named AFL grand final pre-game entertainment
item.description: Pop superstar Kylie Minogue has been named as the men's AFL grand final pre-match entertainment, the first time since 2021 that an Australian artist has headlined the event.

item.title: Live: Iran threatens to retaliate if Pentagon orders ground operation
item.description: US media is reporting that the Pentagon is considering a weeks-long ground campaign in Iran. Follow our live coverage.

item.title: Live: National cabinet to meet for second time over fuel crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will hold a national cabinet meeting with state and territory leaders this morning to discuss a coordinated response to the fuel crisis. Follow live.

item.title: Food charities appeal for federal government help as fuel prices burden volunteers and budgets
item.description: Charities supplying food relief to struggling Australians are grappling with the twin pressures of rising demand for help while their own fuel bills soar.

item.title: Rural families consider sacrificing children's sport, school runs as fuel costs soar
item.description: Rural and remote families who are spending as much as $500 a week on fuel amid the current oil crisis say they are preparing to make tough decisions about whether to make school runs and to go without sport and even health care.

item.title: Scammers rip off Australians to the tune of $2 billion, ACCC figures show
item.description: Australians lost more than $2 billion to scammers in 2025, the latest data from the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has revealed.

item.title: Live: ASX to fall after Wall Street tanks, oil price pressure as Houthis enter war
item.description: Wall Street tumbled as the Middle East situation deteriorated further, and the ASX is set to be hit by the same wave of anxiety. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Sam Kerr and Ellie Carpenter return to spark Chelsea Women's Super League win over Aston Villa
item.description: Sam Kerr scores and sets up another goal with the help of Matildas teammate Ellie Carpenter as the Australians return to London to help Chelsea take down Aston Villa.

item.title: Electric trucks trade diesel for batteries to make their mark on Australian roads
item.description: A Central Coast business is converting diesel trucks to electric, amid rising fuel prices across Australia, with the first end-to-end electric delivery from Sydney to Canberra highlighting the potential of battery-powered freight.

item.title: 'Plenty of blue skies' expected in Queensland over Easter weekend with some 'stop-start rainfall'
item.description: Mostly sunny, cool Easter weather is expected for much of Queensland, with only brief coastal showers, while far-north areas such as Cairns can expect heavier, more persistent rain.

item.title: Farmers, fishers and remote Indigenous communities appeal for fuel price relief and supply solutions
item.description: Farmers, fishers and business leaders are appealing to the federal and state governments to provide targeted relief to help them deal with massive fuel price increases caused by the Iran War, and the looming prospect of shortages.

item.title: Violent organised crime syndicates linked to illegal tobacco may be coming to Darwin
item.description: Convenience stores selling cheap, illegally packaged cigarettes have been popping up across Darwin and businesses are worried that organised crime and gang violence are next.

item.title: WA tourist towns grapple with wildlife loss, damage to businesses after Cyclone Narelle
item.description: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

item.title: Hand-raised baby corals bring hope of restoring Great Barrier Reef
item.description: Tens of thousands of baby corals, grown on land or settled from wild spawn slicks, have been transplanted onto the Great Barrier Reef.
